texto: When a man is standing at the top of a cliff, it isn’t fear of falling that keeps him rooted to the edge — it’s the fear of not being able to resist the desire to jump, just to see what falling feels like.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cristelle Comby: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “When a man is standing at the top of a cliff, it isn’t fear of falling that keeps him rooted to t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, abyss, cliff pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de cliff e desire.

frase_original: When a man is standing at the top of a cliff, it isn’t fear of falling that keeps him rooted to the edge — it’s the fear of not being able to resist the desire to jump, just to see what falling feels like.

texto: As darkness falls and sleep pulls at me, I wonder, not for the first time, about life and death. Both are real and neither would exist without the other. They balance each other, as do night and day. Hot and cold. Summer and winter.Some people struggle with this concept. At times, they even forget all about it. But if you remember just one thing, let it be this: life always balances itself out. For every bad guy out there, there is someone equally good to make things right again.
explicacao: Citação de Cristelle Comby sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “As darkness falls and sleep pulls at me, I wonder, not for the first time, about life and death. …” articula reflexao, balance, death em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

They balance each other, as do night and day. Hot and cold. Summer and winter.Some people struggle with this concept. At times, they even forget all about it. But if you remember just one thing, let it be this: life always balances itself out. For every bad guy out there, there is someone equally good to make things right again.

texto: Life is short, precious, and should not be wasted. Everyone has a chance at it. We’re equals after all. There are no pawns, no kings, and no queens. We’re all humans and we all have the same value.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Cristelle Comby sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Life is short, precious, and should not be wasted. Everyone has a chance at it. We’re equals afte…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, chess, game, útil em momentos de game e human-life.

texto: I’ve always believed that, as long as we’re left alone to do what we’re good at, and be who we’ve decided to be, things will turn out all right in the end. Now, me being strapped to a chair, unable to see what is going on, and Egan coming to the rescue, carrying a freaking bomb. That is not — by a mile — all right.
explicacao: Citação de Cristelle Comby sobre temas ligados a amizade. “I’ve always believed that, as long as we’re left alone to do what we’re good at, and be who we’ve…” articula amizade, bomb, fear em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e fear.

Now, me being strapped to a chair, unable to see what is going on, and Egan coming to the rescue, carrying a freaking bomb. That is not — by a mile — all right.
